# oms-tool — LLM Reference

External research tool. Scans GitHub/Reddit/HN for trending AI tools or evaluates a specific URL, compares against the full OMS+Claude system, identifies gaps, and proposes concrete improvements.

## Triggers — use /oms-tool when

- Checking what's new in the AI tooling ecosystem (weekly radar)
- Evaluating a specific repo, article, or paper for system improvement
- CEO shares a link to something that might be worth adopting
- Monday 09:00 UTC radar scan via Discord #radar channel

## Invocation

```
/oms-tool                      # scan GitHub + Reddit + HN this week
/oms-tool <url>                # evaluate a specific source
/oms-tool <url1> <url2>        # evaluate multiple sources together
/oms-tool [image]              # evaluate a screenshot/article image
```

## What it does

1. Maps the full current system (CLAUDE.md + rules + oms-overview + router)
2. Fetches source content via browse fetch
3. Extracts genuinely novel mechanisms — discards marketing
4. Gap analysis: what they do vs what we do vs gap level
5. Proposes concrete improvements with effort/impact estimates
6. Waits for CEO confirmation before implementing anything

## Output

Gap analysis table + proposed improvements + Summary with Implement/Pick/Skip decision.
Never writes code before CEO confirms.

## Key reads

~/.claude/CLAUDE.md, ~/.claude/oms-overview.md, ~/.claude/agents/router/persona.md
~/.claude/rules/patterns.md, ~/.claude/rules/agents.md, ~/.claude/rules/performance.md
